_comment: Video Factory configuration schema. Unit master switches (Unit-Scout, Unit-Data, etc.) gate entire pipelines. All nested *_config blocks are only read when the parent Unit-* switch is true.

topic.description: Topic for video (e.g., 'Programming Language', 'AI Frameworks'). Can be a debate motion when Unit-Debate=true. When packaging_config.video_style=["ytid"], set this to a YouTube video ID (e.g. 'WnS4zE4wOtQ') — the metadata tool fetches title/description/tags from that existing video. Set to 'auto' to let the scout pipeline pick the next topic from the output queue automatically (requires Unit-Scout=true).

special_values.auto: Pull next queued topic from scout output queue. Requires Unit-Scout=true and a populated queue. The scout writes candidate topics to a JSON queue file; 'auto' pops the top-ranked entry and uses it as the topic for this run.

video_formats.description: Video formats to generate. Applies to all tools: bar_race, intro, definition_video, debate_video.

video_style.description: Top-level video style tag(s) used by the packaging pipeline. Mirrors packaging_config.video_style and controls which branch the metadata tool takes. Valid values: 'debate', 'bar_race', 'animation', 'ytid'.

fps.description: Seconds per period for bar_race videos. Controls animation pace — higher = slower/longer video.

fps_hd_offset.description: Extra seconds per period for HD only. Shorts uses fps; HD uses fps + fps_hd_offset.

video_fps.description: Output frame rate for ALL video tools (intro, bar_race, definition_video, debate_video). Must be identical across all tools — mismatched fps causes A/V sync failure on merge.
video_fps.tip: 30fps recommended. Never mix fps values — all segment tools read this single field.

audio_speed.description: Speech speed for Shorts audio via ffmpeg atempo. 1.0=normal, <1.0=slower, >1.0=faster.
audio_speed.tip: 1.1 = slightly faster. Good for Shorts where narration must be concise.

audio_speed_hd.description: Speech speed for HD audio only. Set 0.0 to fall back to audio_speed for all formats.

examples.0.0: No override — HD uses same speed as audio_speed
examples.0.9: Slightly slower than Shorts — good for longer HD narration

tts_engine.description: TTS engine used for ALL video audio generation (definition_video, debate_video, and future tools). Set once here — applies globally.

options.gtts: Google Text-to-Speech. Free, offline-friendly. Install: pip install gTTS
options.edge-tts: Microsoft Edge Neural TTS. High-quality neural voices. Install: pip install edge-tts. Uses edge_tts_voices config. Falls back to gtts if not installed.
options.piper: Local offline neural TTS via ONNX models. Fastest, no internet needed. Install: pip install piper-tts. Uses piper_voices config. Falls back to gtts if not installed.

audio_lang.description: Language code for gTTS narration (non-debate audio tools: bar_race, definition_video). Only applies when tts_engine=gtts.

audio_tld.description: Top-level domain for gTTS accent selection. Only applies when tts_engine=gtts and audio_lang=en.

use_label_mappings.description: Apply label_mappings.json to shorten bar names (e.g. 'JavaScript' → 'JS').

channel.description: Channel name used in audio narration, intro clip, agent backstory, and metadata. No @ prefix.

channel_lower.description: Lowercase channel slug used in LinkedIn and other platform URLs.

website.description: Channel website or primary URL shown in YouTube metadata.

watermark_enabled.description: Overlay semi-transparent watermark text on video frames.

watermark_text.description: Watermark text displayed on video. Usually '@ChannelName'.

_unit_switches_comment: Master pipeline switches. Set false to hard-skip that unit — no lock, no meta check, no execution.

Unit-Scout.description: Master switch for Unit-Scout. When true, scans configured platforms and niches for viral/trending content and populates a topic queue. When topic='auto', the next queued topic is consumed automatically. When false, scout_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Data.description: Master switch for Unit-Data. Generates all base content: CSV data, debate scripts (.md), definition text. All other content units depend on its output. When false, unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-LeadData.description: Master switch for Unit-LeadData. Collects business leads from Google Maps (via SerpAPI) using the topic as keyword(s) — comma-separate for multiple keywords. Normalizes phone/email/URL, deduplicates, scores quality 0–100, segments hot/warm/cold, and exports CSV/JSON. When false, leaddata_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Debate.description: Master switch for Unit-Debate. Renders debate video from propose.md + oppose.md + decide.md produced by Unit-Data. When false, debate_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Definition.description: Master switch for Unit-Definition. Renders scrolling definition video from definition .md files produced by Unit-Data. When false, unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Animation.description: Master switch for Unit-Animation. Renders bar race and other animation videos from CSV produced by Unit-Data. When false, animation_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Comparison.description: Master switch for Unit-Comparison. Generates comparison content. When false, unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Packaging.description: Master switch for Unit-Packaging. Generates YouTube metadata (title, description, tags, translations), CC subtitles, and thumbnails — everything needed to package the video before upload. When false, packaging_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Publisher.description: Master switch for Unit-Publisher. Uploads final videos to YouTube and/or Facebook. Requires Unit-Packaging output to exist. When false, publisher_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

Unit-Advertise.description: Master switch for Unit-Advertise. Creates promotional derivatives (Shorts cuts, social clips, TVC) from finished videos and posts to social platforms. When false, advertise_config is ignored and the unit is skipped entirely.

leaddata_config_file.description: Path to external Unit-LeadData config JSON. Auto-loaded by config_loader (Rule 37) and injected as inputs['leaddata_config']. The referenced file's content IS the leaddata_config block — no outer wrapper needed (same pattern as clips3d.json).

scout_config.description: Trend Scout pipeline settings. Only read when Unit-Scout=true.

force_scraping.description: Force URL scraping even if cached results exist. Useful to refresh stale data.

platforms.description: Platforms to scout for trending topics. 'scraping_url' triggers scraping from URLs defined in scraping_url file.

niches.description: Topic niches / keywords to filter trending content by. Only posts/videos matching these themes are considered.

min_virality_score.description: Minimum virality score (0–100) a candidate topic must reach to be added to the queue. Higher = stricter filtering.

output_queue_size.description: Maximum number of topics to keep in the output queue at once. Oldest entries are evicted when the queue is full.

auto_consume.description: When true and topic='auto', automatically pop the top-ranked topic from the queue and set it as the run topic without manual intervention.

llm_scout.description: LLM for the trend scout agent used to score and rank candidate topics. null=project default.

use_web_search.description: Allow the scout agent to perform live web searches to validate and enrich candidate topics before scoring.

scraping_url.description: Path to a JSON file listing URLs to scrape for trending content. Used when 'scraping_url' is included in platforms.

social_credentials_file.description: Path to social platform credentials JSON for reading platform feeds (LinkedIn, Instagram, etc.).

fb_credentials_file.description: Path to Facebook credentials JSON for reading Facebook Page/Group feeds.

yt_client_secrets_file.description: Path to YouTube OAuth2 client_secrets.json for reading YouTube trending data.

yt_token_file.description: Path to saved YouTube OAuth2 token. Auto-created after first browser login.

leaddata_config.description: Lead data collection pipeline settings. Only read when Unit-LeadData=true. Topic field provides search keywords (comma-separated for multiple). Pipeline: collect → normalize → score → export.

enabled.description: Inner enable flag. When false, the unit returns 'disabled' even if Unit-LeadData=true. Useful for keeping the config block intact while temporarily disabling.

sources.description: Lead data sources to query. 'maps' = Google Maps via SerpAPI (primary). 'csv' = local CSV at input/leads.csv (fallback for testing).

collect_config.description: Lead collection settings. Controls how raw leads are fetched from external sources.

credentials_file.description: Filename of SerpAPI credentials JSON. Resolved to .runtime/secrets/{filename}. File content: { "api_key": "..." }. Never commit.

api_endpoint.description: SerpAPI search endpoint. Override only if using a regional/proxy mirror.

engine.description: SerpAPI engine. 'google_maps' returns local_results with phone/address/website/coordinates.

search_type.description: Maps search subtype. 'search' = standard place search.

request_timeout.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds for each SerpAPI call.

max_results_per_keyword.description: Maximum number of leads to extract per keyword. SerpAPI returns up to 20 local_results per call.

skip_if_cached.description: When true and raw/leads_raw.csv already exists in the workspace, skip the collect step and reuse the cached file. Set false to force a fresh fetch.

normalize_config.description: Normalization and deduplication settings. Standardizes phone/email/URL formatting and removes duplicate leads.

deduplicate_on.description: Field(s) used to detect duplicate leads via hash key. Most reliable: ['phone']. Stricter: ['phone', 'email'].

phone_country_default.description: Country prefix to prepend to phones missing a '+'. Empty string = prepend '+' only. Format: '+971' for UAE, '+880' for BD, etc.

lowercase_email.description: Force email addresses to lowercase before storing. Disable only if you need raw casing preserved.

force_https.description: Prepend 'https://' to website URLs missing a scheme. When false, prepends 'http://' instead.

strip_unicode.description: Apply NFD unicode normalization to text fields (name, address, location, category) for consistent searchability.

min_name_length.description: Reject leads whose normalized name is shorter than this many characters. Filters out junk results.

score_config.description: Quality scoring and segmentation settings. Each lead gets a 0–100 score based on data completeness, then bucketed into hot/warm/cold.

score_enabled.description: When false, all leads receive score=0 and segment='cold'. Useful for skipping scoring on already-scored datasets.

scoring_rubric.description: Point values awarded for each present field. Sum is capped at 100.

has_phone.description: Points awarded when phone is present.

has_email.description: Points awarded when email is present.

has_website.description: Points awarded when website is present.

has_address.description: Points awarded when address is present (proxy for premium/verified location).

active_business.description: Points awarded when last_verified timestamp exists (active record).

segment_thresholds.description: Score boundaries for each segment. score >= hot → 'hot'; score >= warm → 'warm'; otherwise → 'cold'.

hot.description: Minimum score for hot segment (ready to contact).

cold.description: Minimum score for cold segment. Always 0.

sort_by_score_desc.description: Sort scored CSV/JSON output by quality_score descending (highest first). Disable to preserve collection order.

formats.description: Final export formats. CSV is always written; 'json' adds a parallel JSON file. Reserved: 'jsonl', 'parquet'.

generate_stats.description: Generate insights/lead_stats.json with totals, fill rates, and segment breakdown. Disable to skip the analytics step.

stats_file.description: Filename for the stats JSON written to insights/. Override only if multiple runs share an insights directory.

include_segments_breakdown.description: When true, add a per-segment count map ({hot:N, warm:N, cold:N}) to the stats file.

animation_config.description: Animation pipeline settings. Only read when Unit-Animation=true.

use_existing_csv.description: Skip research+CSV generation and use existing output/{filename}.csv. Auto-generates if CSV missing.

definition_enabled.description: Generate plain-English topic definition saved to output/{filename}.txt.

use_existing_definition.description: Use existing output/{filename}.txt instead of generating a new one.

definition_max_chars.description: Maximum characters for the topic definition text.

definition_video.description: Create scrolling text video from definition .txt file. Output: definition_video_{fmt}_with_audio.mp4.

intro_enabled.description: Generate branded intro clip for the animation pipeline. Output: intro_{fmt}_with_audio.mp4. Controlled independently per pipeline block — does not affect debate intro.

intro_duration.description: Intro clip duration in seconds for Shorts/portrait formats (animation pipeline).

intro_duration_hd.description: Intro clip duration in seconds for HD/landscape formats (animation pipeline). Set 0 to use intro_duration for all.

bar_race_video_enabled.description: Generate bar race video. Output: bar_race_{fmt}_with_audio.mp4.

bar_race_audio_enabled.description: Generate TTS audio narration specifically for bar race videos. Reads CSV data to create data-driven commentary synced to the animation. Output: bar_race_{fmt}_audio.mp3 and bar_race_cc_en.txt. Independent of audio_enabled.

bar_merge_enabled.description: Concatenate intro + bar_race + definition_video into final video per format. Output: {channel}_{topic_slug}_{fmt}.mp4.
bar_merge_enabled.note: All 3 segment _with_audio.mp4 files must exist and have identical video_fps.

video_enabled.description: Enable standard video generation via smart_video_tool.

audio_enabled.description: Enable audio narration for standard (non-bar-race) videos.

merge_audio_video.description: Merge standard audio with standard video files.

llm_researcher.description: LLM for data_researcher agent. null=project default.

llm_definition.description: LLM for definition_specialist agent. null=project default.

llm_csv.description: LLM for csv_generator agent. null=project default.

llm_video.description: LLM for video_producer and bar_race_video_producer agents. null=project default.

llm_audio.description: LLM for audio_engineer agent. null=project default.

intro_slug.description: Custom subtitle line shown in the animation intro clip. Replaces the default second line of the intro.
intro_slug.example: Watch Race how the leaders change over time

debate_config.description: Debate pipeline settings. Only read when Unit-Debate=true.

use_label_mappings.description: Apply label_mappings.json to shorten bar names in debate context (e.g. 'JavaScript' → 'JS'). Mirrors the top-level use_label_mappings but scoped to the debate pipeline only.

debate_definition_enabled.description: Generate debate text (propose.md, oppose.md, decide.md) via LLM agents. Smart skip: if all 3 .md files already exist, LLM generation is skipped automatically.

debate_video_enabled.description: Render debate video from propose.md + oppose.md + decide.md. Output: debate_video_{fmt}_with_audio.mp4.

debate_3d_enabled.description: Render the debate video using a 3D scene/effect instead of the standard 2D scrolling text layout. When true, the debate_video_tool switches to a 3D rendering mode. Requires additional dependencies.

debate_merge_enabled.description: Concatenate debate_video segments into final video per format. Output: {channel}_{topic_slug}_{fmt}.mp4.

intro_slug.description: Custom subtitle line shown in the debate intro clip (if intro_enabled=true). Replaces the default second line.
intro_slug.example: One of the biggest debates in tech right now

intro_enabled.description: Generate branded intro clip for the debate pipeline. Output: intro_{fmt}_with_audio.mp4. Controlled independently per pipeline block — does not affect animation intro.

intro_duration.description: Intro clip duration in seconds for Shorts/portrait formats (debate pipeline).

intro_duration_hd.description: Intro clip duration in seconds for HD/landscape formats (debate pipeline). Set 0 to use intro_duration for all.

debate_secs_per_line.description: Seconds each line is shown as active in the debate video.

debate_max_chars.description: Maximum characters for each debate section (propose, oppose, decide). Hard cap applied by DebateDefinitionTool.

llm_debate.description: LLM for debater and judge agents. null=project default.

piper_voices.description: Per-role voice config for tts_engine=piper. Only read when tts_engine=piper. Install: pip install piper-tts. Download models from https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices

edge_tts_voices.description: Per-role neural voice config for tts_engine=edge-tts. 5 roles: propose, oppose, judge_f (Female Judge/SUMMARY), judge_m (Male Judge/ANALYSIS), decide (Chief Judge/DECISION).

decide.description: Voice for DECISION section — Chief Judge. Declares the winner. Used for mod_verdict block.

judge_f.description: Voice for SUMMARY section — Female Judge. Reads both sides summary. Recommended: en-CA-ClaraNeural (Canadian, ~35, neutral).

judge_m.description: Voice for ANALYSIS section — Male Judge. Provides analytical commentary. Recommended: en-CA-LiamNeural (Canadian, ~35, neutral).

debate_bg_opacity.description: Background image opacity for debate video overlay. 0=transparent, 255=opaque.

debate_background_enabled.description: Generate an AI-created background image and composite it behind the debate text. Requires image_gen_backend to be configured.

debate_background_prompt.description: Text prompt for AI background image generation. Keep cinematic and abstract — avoid text, people, and faces.
debate_background_prompt.example: futuristic digital debate arena, dark navy deep purple, abstract geometric circuit patterns, soft volumetric light beams, blurred bokeh background, cinematic, no text, no people, no faces

_clips_folder_structure.description: Debate video clips folder structure when _folder_prefix=true. Each segment has a numbered folder (00-18) containing HD and Shorts variants.
_clips_folder_structure.lookup_mechanism: When _folder_prefix=true, scans assets/clips/ for folders ending with _{key}. E.g., 'sum' finds '14_sum', 'intro' finds '00_intro'. Falls back to assets/clips/{key}/ if not found.

pipeline_usage.Shorts: Uses p0-p3, c0-c3 (max 4 args per side)
pipeline_usage.HD: Uses p0-p5, c0-c5 (max 6 args per side)

image_gen_backend.description: Backend for AI image generation when debate_background_enabled=true.

options.auto: Tries backends in order: replicate → stability → openai → local

debate_mini_enabled.description: Generate short/mini debate version (-m.md files) for Shorts format. When true, debate_propose_m / debate_oppose_m / debate_decide_m tasks run to produce compressed debate scripts.

debate_mini_max_chars.description: Maximum characters for each mini debate section (-m.md files used for Shorts). Should be significantly smaller than debate_max_chars.

debate_3d_clips.description: Background clip configuration for the 3D debate renderer (debate_3d_enabled=true). Maps each pipeline segment key to one or more video/image sources. Supports multi-clip sequences with intro paths (played once) and loop clips (cycled indefinitely). Config is the single source of truth — no static fallback directories.
debate_3d_clips.format_note: All path values are relative to PROJECT_ROOT. Missing files resolve to None — renderer shows placeholder frame.

single_path_loop.description: Intro clip plays once, then loop clip cycles.

multi_path_loop.description: Multiple intro clips each played once in order, then loops cycled.

image_with_frames.description: Static image held for exactly N frames, then advances to next path entry.

mixed_paths.description: Image intro (held 90 frames) followed by video clip, then loop.

image_support.frames_note: IMAGE_DEFAULT_HOLD=90 frames (~3s at 30fps). Override per entry with frames key.

intro.description: Intro clip with optional subtext subtitle. Plays when intro_enabled=true or when clip config path exists. Must include subtext for subtitle overlay.

ads_primary.description: Ad clip played after intro. Set null to disable. Must include subtext.

ads_secondary.description: Ad clip played before mod_verdict. Must include subtext.

subscribe.description: Subscribe CTA clip played last. Must include subtext.

p0_to_p5.description: Proposition argument background clips. p0=opening, p1-p5=arguments. Shorts uses p0-p3; HD uses p0-p5. Missing keys render placeholder frame.

c0_to_c5.description: Opposition argument background clips. c0=opening, c1-c5=arguments.

mod_summary.description: Background for Female Judge SUMMARY section (## SUMMARY in decide.md). Voice: judge_f (en-CA-ClaraNeural).

mod_analysis.description: Background for Male Judge ANALYSIS section (## ANALYSIS in decide.md). Voice: judge_m (en-CA-LiamNeural).

mod_verdict.description: Background for Chief Judge DECISION section (## DECISION in decide.md). Voice: decide (en-US-ChristopherNeural).

Shorts.description: Clip map for Shorts format (1080x1920 portrait). All block clips use TTS audio — no subtext needed. Only intro, ads, subscribe use subtext.

intro.subtext: Welcome 360Debate! See Every Side, Decide Smarter.

HD.description: Clip map for HD format (1920x1080 landscape). Same structure as Shorts with additional p4/c4/p5/c5 keys.

lang_suffix.description: Auto-injected at runtime from CLI flag (e.g. -bn → 'Bn'). Do NOT set this manually in data.json — it is always derived from the CLI flag. Defaults to 'En' when no flag is given.

_lang_override_files.description: To run in a different language, create input/dataXx.json (where Xx = capitalized ISO code). Only fields that differ from data.json need to be included — the override is deep-merged on top of data.json.
_lang_override_files.cli: crewai run -{xx}  — e.g.  crewai run -bn  |  crewai run -fr  |  crewai run -ar

minimal_override_example._comment: Bengali override — only fields that differ from data.json

packaging_config.description: Metadata & thumbnail preparation settings. Only read when Unit-Packaging=true. Generates YouTube metadata (title, description, tags, CC subtitles, translations) and AI thumbnail — everything needed to package the video before upload.

video_style.description: Pipeline type to generate metadata for. Controls which branch the metadata tool takes.

enum_notes.debate: Generates metadata from debate pipeline output files
enum_notes.bar_race: Generates metadata from bar race animation output
enum_notes.animation: Generates metadata from standard animation pipeline
enum_notes.ytid: Fetches metadata from an existing YouTube video — set topic to the video ID (e.g. 'WnS4zE4wOtQ')

generate_youtube_metadata.description: Generate YouTube metadata (title, description, tags, chapters) and translate to 31 languages. Output: YT/{fmt}/MD/ and YT/{fmt}/CC/.

generate_thumbnail.description: Generate AI thumbnail image for YouTube. Output: {topic_slug}.jpg and {topic_slug}.png in output/{slug}/YT/{fmt}/Th/. Alias: generate_yt_thumbnail also accepted.

generate_yt_thumbnail.description: Alias for generate_thumbnail. Use generate_thumbnail instead.

yt_metadata_lang.description: Number of languages to generate YouTube metadata translations (title, description) for. String-encoded integer.

yt_cc_lang.description: Number of languages to generate CC subtitle translations for. String-encoded integer.

video_formats.description: Video format subdirectory names to generate metadata for. Use 'debate' for debate pipeline output.
video_formats.note: Use ['HD','Shorts'] for animation pipeline, ['debate'] for debate pipeline.

llm_youtube.description: LLM for youtube_metadata_specialist agent. null=project default.

publisher_config.description: Upload destination settings. Only read when Unit-Publisher=true. Sub-blocks (yt_upload_config, fb_upload_config) controlled by their own yt_upload / fb_upload switches.

llm_upload.description: LLM for youtube_upload_specialist agent. null=project default.

llm_embed.description: LLM for embedding tasks (semantic search). null=project default.

yt_upload_config.description: YouTube upload settings. Only active when yt_upload=true.

upload_youtube_video.description: Upload final merged videos to YouTube via Data API v3. Smart skip: if upload_log.json already contains video_id, re-upload is skipped.
upload_youtube_video.note: Requires client_secrets.json from Google Cloud Console. First run opens browser for OAuth2 login.

upload_dry_run.description: Dry run mode — validates that video files, metadata, and CC files exist without uploading anything to YouTube. No API calls made. Useful for testing before real upload.

upload_cc.description: Upload CC subtitle files from YT/{fmt}/CC/ to YouTube after video upload. Covers all 31 languages.

upload_cc_lang.description: Max number of CC subtitle languages to upload to YouTube (0 or omit = upload all). String-encoded integer. Independent from yt_cc_lang (generation count).

upload_md_lang.description: Max number of MD localization languages to upload to YouTube (0 or omit = upload all). String-encoded integer. Independent from yt_metadata_lang (generation count).

upload_notify_subscribers.description: Notify channel subscribers when video is uploaded. Recommended false for private/unlisted.

upload_client_secrets_file.description: Path to OAuth2 client_secrets.json from Google Cloud Console.
upload_client_secrets_file.setup: https://console.cloud.google.com → APIs & Services → Credentials → Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID → Desktop App → Download JSON

upload_token_file.description: Path to saved OAuth2 token. Auto-created after first browser login. Reused on subsequent runs.

yt_pin_comment_gen.description: Auto-generate and pin a comment on the video after upload using LLM.

fb_upload_config.description: Facebook upload settings. Only active when fb_upload=true. Shorts → Facebook Reel. HD → Facebook Video. Smart skip: if fb_upload_log.json already contains video_id, re-upload is skipped.

upload_facebook_video.description: Upload video directly to Facebook Page via Graph API.
upload_facebook_video.note: Requires input/fb_credentials.json with page_id and access_token.

privacy_status.tip: Use SELF (only me) for testing before setting EVERYONE for public.

credentials_file.description: Path to Facebook credentials JSON. Must contain page_id and access_token.

advertise_config.description: Social sharing and promotional derivative settings. Only read when Unit-Advertise=true. Posts the YouTube video URL as a link post to configured platforms — completely independent of publisher/fb_upload. Reads upload_log.json directly.

social_share_enabled.description: Post uploaded YouTube video URL + thumbnail to configured social platforms.

social_share_dry_run.description: Simulate social posts without actually posting. Logs what would be posted.

social_platforms.description: Platforms to post to. Credentials read from input/social_credentials.json.
social_platforms.tip: Facebook here = link post to FB Page (not a video upload). YouTube posts a Community post. Instagram requires a publicly accessible image URL.

schedule_post.description: Wait until schedule_datetime before posting to social platforms. The crew sleeps in 60-second chunks with countdown logs until the target time is reached. If the scheduled time has already passed, posts immediately.

schedule_datetime.description: Target date and time to post. Interpreted in schedule_timezone.

schedule_timezone.description: IANA timezone string for schedule_datetime. Used with Python's zoneinfo module (Python 3.9+).

llm_social.description: LLM for social_share_specialist agent. null=project default.

_llm_examples.description: LLM string format reference for all llm_* fields

examples.openai/gpt-4o-mini: OpenAI GPT-4o Mini — fast and cheap
examples.dashscope/qwen-plus: Alibaba Qwen Plus — good for structured text

intro_enabled.description: DEPRECATED at top level. Set inside animation_config and/or debate_config instead. Each pipeline block controls its own intro independently — intro_enabled=true in an active block triggers the intro clip for that pipeline only. OR logic: if any active block has intro_enabled=true, the intro runs.

intro_duration.description: DEPRECATED at top level. Set inside animation_config and/or debate_config instead.

intro_duration_hd.description: DEPRECATED at top level. Set inside animation_config and/or debate_config instead. Set 0 to use intro_duration for all formats.
